channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Six-year-old girl dies after falling from 30th-floor window of Toronto apartment building
item.description: A six-year-old girl has died after falling from a high-rise apartment building in Toronto, according to police. Emergency services were called at approximately 8:15 p.m. on Sunday to the area of Chalk...

item.title: Amber Heard focuses on family life in Spain while Johnny Depp returns to Hollywood
item.description: Amber Heard has embraced a quieter life in Madrid, Spain, focusing on her three young children, who were born via surrogacy. Since her relocation, she has largely withdrawn from the celebrity scene, p...

item.title: Alexandre Boulerice leaves NDP to join Québec solidaire in provincial election
item.description: Alexandre Boulerice has officially announced his resignation from the federal New Democratic Party (NDP) to join the sovereigntist Québec solidaire party ahead of the October provincial election. Boul...

item.title: South Africa's Broken Promises: Decades of Unfulfilled Potential
item.description: Thirty-two years after the dawn of democracy, the promise of a 'better life for all' remains a painfully hollow echo for many South Africans. The initial optimism surrounding the Reconstruction and De...

item.title: Lagos Flood Alert 2026: 17 Areas to Avoid as Heavy Rainfall Approaches
item.description: Lagos is bracing for above-normal rainfall in 2026, with predictions indicating up to 3,030 mm of rain from March to December. This rainfall, along with 'tidal locks' and dam releases, is expected to ...

item.title: Labor outlines initial workplace AI regulations without union veto powers
item.description: The Albanese government has taken its first formal steps towards regulating artificial intelligence (AI) in Australian workplaces, signalling a cautious but deliberate approach. Workplace Relations Mi...

item.title: Court allows 10-day detention of suspect over alleged hate speech and incitement in viral video case
item.description: A Kenyan court has authorised the detention of a suspect for 10 days to allow the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) to complete inquiries into allegations of hate speech and ethnic inciteme...

item.title: Ramaphosa warns against corruption while reflecting on South Africa’s democratic progress and ongoing challenges
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa used his Freedom Day address in Bloemfontein to strongly condemn corruption, warning that the misuse of public funds undermines South Africa’s democracy. He stressed that eve...

item.title: Ramaphosa reflects on democratic progress and service delivery challenges during Freedom Day address
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa used his Freedom Day address in Bloemfontein to reflect on South Africa’s democratic journey, emphasising both the progress made since the end of apartheid and the pressing c...

item.title: Davao City ranks top in the Philippines for travel experiences
item.description: Davao City has once again been recognized as the top city in the Philippines for travel, according to the World Travel Index (WTI) April 2026 rankings. The city scored an overall rating of 75.66, slig...

item.title: Allegations of Lord Mandelson's vetting role prompt scrutiny of Keir Starmer
item.description: The article, seemingly reporting on a developing story, explores claims regarding the vetting process for peerages, specifically involving Lord Mandelson and its potential implications for Keir Starme...

item.title: A Guide to Choosing Outdoor Patio Furniture for All Spaces and Budgets
item.description: Creating a comfortable and stylish outdoor space is easier than ever with a range of patio furniture options designed to suit any style and budget. Compact bistro sets, like the 3-piece aluminum set, ...

item.title: Toronto police union urges province to reconsider Gardiner Expressway and DVP changes amid officer concerns
item.description: The head of Toronto’s police union is calling on the Ontario government to reconsider proposed changes affecting the Gardiner Expressway and the Don Valley Parkway (DVP), arguing that the adjustments ...

item.title: Trump and Melania criticise Jimmy Kimmel’s remarks and urge ABC to take action after controversial joke
item.description: US President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump have strongly criticised late-night host Jimmy Kimmel over a controversial joke made during his show, calling on ABC and its parent company Disne...

item.title: Father faints during his wife’s C-section childbirth in Brazil
item.description: A father-of-two in Brazil fainted while witnessing the birth of his second daughter during a Caesarean section at a hospital in Criciúma. The incident occurred on 18 April, when Maicon Pedroso was hol...

item.title: Supreme Court to Hear Pension Claim of Second Wife After First Wife’s Demise
item.description: In a recent development, the Supreme Court of India has agreed to examine the issue of whether a second wife can claim pensionary benefits following the death of the first wife of a deceased governmen...

item.title: APC Updates 2027 Election Schedule With New Dates and Procedures
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don come out with revised timetable and schedule for the 2027 General Elections. This new timetable, wey the Party National Organising Secretary, Sulaiman Muhammad ...

item.title: Northern Ireland First Minister condemns car bomb attack and vows progress
item.description: The First Minister of Northern Ireland has issued a strong statement following a recent car bomb incident, emphasising that the region will not allow itself to regress into past conflicts. The attack,...

item.title: 72-year-old mother sues millionaire son over alleged £2 million family financial dispute in High Court
item.description: A 72-year-old reiki therapist, crystal healer and angel card reader named Moya Montgomerie is engaged in a bitter High Court dispute with her 55-year-old millionaire son, Jason Minns, and his wife Ste...

item.title: Delhi announces Heatwave Action Plan 2026 with ORS in schools and cooling measures in public services
item.description: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta has announced the Heatwave Action Plan 2026 as the national capital continues to face intense summer temperatures reaching above 40°C. The plan includes a series of pr...
